name: Mo Amer
bio: Mo Amer is a Palestinian-American stand-up comedian and actor who fled Kuwait as a child during the Gulf War and grew up in Houston, drawing on his refugee experience and Arab-American identity for warm, story-driven comedy. He toured with the trio Allah Made Me Funny and with Dave Chappelle before releasing the Netflix specials The Vagabond, Mohammed in Texas, and Wild World. He created and stars in the acclaimed Netflix series Mo, which won a Peabody Award.

timeline.ev: Reaches finals of Houston's Funniest Person Contest
Tours nationally with the Allah Made Me Funny trio
Tours with Dave Chappelle; records Legally Homeless
Releases first Netflix special, The Vagabond
Creates and stars in the Netflix series Mo; appears in Black Adam
Releases third Netflix special, Wild World

accolades: Peabody Award for the Netflix series Mo (2023)
Gotham Award, Breakthrough Series Short Form for Mo (2022)
Among the first Arab-Americans with a nationally televised stand-up special
